title: And to all the children Congratulations
display_title: And to all the children Congratulations
wikitext:
[]: And to all the children... Congratulations!             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 12, 2007 8:08 pm I just had a thought. When Shinji/the narrator at the end of "The Beast that Shouted [Word] at the Heart of the World" congratulates "the children", could the congratulation be, perhaps, more than just to himself, Rei, and Asuka? To quote Anno from his interview in "Let's Die Together": Hideaki T. Anno wrote: "I don't see any adults here in Japan. [...] We are a country of children."

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Nov 12, 2007 8:14 pm I think he very well could have been addressing Japan I always thought that he was addressing the viewers who got their bubbles of safety shattered by Eva

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 12, 2007 8:27 pm I think that line (which I guess is spoken by Shinji seeing as "Thankyou, father" and "Goodbye, mother" also go along with him) can be seen as a line to humanity in Eva or the real world as a whole. Ya know, the whole "life ain't that bad so try to live it the best you can and become happy" deal.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Nov 12, 2007 8:38 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I think that line (which I guess is spoken by Shinji seeing as "Thankyou, father" and "Goodbye, mother" also go along with him) can be seen as a line to humanity in Eva or the real world as a whole. Ya know, the whole "life ain't that bad so try to live it the best you can and become happy" deal.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Nov 12, 2007 8:42 pm I always thought "mother" and "father" were addressed to his real parents. And to be honest, I always thought "to all the children" was addressed to the rest of Japan. I never realized people thought of it another way than that.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Nov 12, 2007 9:35 pm Aside from Shinji, I thought the congratulations was to the viewer for sitting through 25 and 26 without inflicting serious bodily harm on themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 12, 2007 9:50 pm remember, kids: Anno=Shinji. From what I understand... Goodbye to my Mother=parallel to Shinji's line in EoE ("Goodbye, Mother.") Thankyou to my Father: Possibly seen as a reply to what Gendou said in EoE. ("I'm sorry...Shinji." /my two cents.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Nov 12, 2007 9:50 pm I always took that to be addressed to the world. In truth, we're a world of children, despite how much we like playing grown-up.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Nov 12, 2007 10:04 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I always took that to be addressed to the world. In truth, we're a world of children, despite how much we like playing grown-up.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 12, 2007 10:05 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Goodbye to my Mother=parallel to Shinji's line in EoE ("Goodbye, Mother.") Thankyou to my Father: Possibly seen as a reply to what Gendou said in EoE. ("I'm sorry...Shinji."

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 12, 2007 10:07 pm According to some interview with him, Anno said the relationship between Gendou and Shinji WAS (loosely) based on his own strained relationship with his father.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Nov 12, 2007 10:12 pm EvaCub wrote: thats funny I just came to that conclusion myself yesterday

[]:             Quote Post by Sheer Heart Attack » Mon Nov 12, 2007 10:14 pm NAveryW wrote: But did Anno truly lose his mother and loathe his father?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 12, 2007 10:54 pm I can't the online SAUCE I'm thinking of, but a written source that comes to mind is from here: Animerica, July 2002, Vol.10 No.7: Animerica Spotlight: Neon Genesis Evangelion wrote: To be fair, Eva does seem to contain a host of personal symbols. It is said that the troubled relationship between Shinji and and his distant manipulative father is based on Anno's own childhood. But the lasting success of Evangelion seems to indicate that by somehow trying to create a very personal work, Anno wound up tapping into something very universal.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Nov 13, 2007 4:35 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I always took that to be addressed to the world. In truth, we're a world of children, despite how much we like playing grown-up.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Nov 13, 2007 5:04 am Alaska Slim wrote: If everyone is a child, then "children" themselves must not exist, as we are therefore all the same with no "adults" in the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Tue Nov 13, 2007 5:24 am I always thought of the mother and father as a double meaning. It is Anno thanking his parents and Shinji thanking his. As for the children, I never once thought it was just Shinji, Asuka and Rei being congratulated. I always thought he was congratulating the children of Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Nov 13, 2007 9:10 am However, because NGE is a kind of political statement, I see the 'father mother' as our ancestors. We are the children of them, and we have to fix the way now, or else we would have failed our ancestors.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Nov 13, 2007 9:36 am I could wrong (since I haven't watched it yet), but isn't the "Children" bit a reference to Space Runaway Ideon?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Nov 13, 2007 10:14 am Shin-seiki wrote: I could wrong (since I haven't watched it yet), but isn't the "Children" bit a reference to Space Runaway Ideon?
